zh.lifeSummary: 當John  William Pope於1529年出生在英國英格蘭奧可福爾迪丁頓時，他的父親Sir Newell Edgar Pope25歲，母親Sharley Kinkey25歲。他和Margaret Woodward至少有4個兒子和4個女兒。他去世於1593年在英國英格蘭薩默塞特享年64歲。

nameMeanings.name: Pope
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English pope (derived via Old English from Late Latin papa ‘bishop, pope’, from Greek pappas ‘father’, in origin a nursery word.) In the early Christian Church, the Latin term was at first used as a title of respect for male clergy of every rank, but in the Western Church it gradually came to be restricted to bishops, and then only to the bishop of Rome; in the Eastern Church it continued to be used of all priests (see Popov , Papas ). The nickname would have been used for a vain or pompous man, or for someone who had played the part of the pope in a pageant or play. The surname is also present in Ireland and Scotland.

variants.name: Popov
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Nikolay, Oleg, Grigoriy, Anatoly, Andrei, Arkadi, Dmitriy, Mikhail, Sergey.  Bulgarian or Macedonian Atanas, Dimitar, Yordan.  Serbian Bosko, Bozidar, Branko.

Russian, Bulgarian, Macedonian, Serbian, and Rusyn: patronymic from pop ‘priest’, from Greek pappas ‘father’ (compare English Pope ). The name may sometimes derive from a nickname, but celibacy was not enjoined on priests of the Orthodox Church and so the name can indeed mean ‘son of the priest’. In many cases, however, the Russian name means no more than ‘of the priest’ and designates a servant of a priest. Compare Popoff , see also Pop 2, Popovic , and Popovski 1.
Jewish (American): most likely a shortened form of   Popovski   2. Compare   Popoff  .

variants.name: Papke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Otto, Willi.
North German (also Päpke): nickname from a diminutive of Low German pape, Dutch paepe ‘pope’. Compare Pope .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.description: Henry VIII dies, succeeded by the boy king Edward VI. England and Ireland were ruled by the senior nobility of England.

en.lifeSummary: When John  William Pope was born in 1529, in Deddington, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Newell Edgar Pope, was 25 and his mother, Sharley Kinkey, was 25. He had at least 4 sons and 4 daughters with Margaret Woodward. He died in 1593, in Somerset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 64.

ko.lifeSummary: John  William Pope(이)가 1529년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 데딩턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Newell Edgar Pope(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Sharley Kinkey(은)는 25세였다. 그는 Margaret Woodward(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1593년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.
